agents.heading: Worker agents
agents.description: Inventory the workboard pool currently sees. Filesystem rows shadow same-slug DB rows; legacy DB rows still present here haven't been edited or aren't yet picked up by the merger.
agents.actions.configureTitle: Edit pool concurrency caps and worker type defaults
agents.table.name: Name
agents.toggle.enabledTitle: Enabled — click to disable. The pool will stop claiming new tasks for this agent.
agents.toggle.disabledTitle: Disabled — click to enable. The pool will resume claiming tasks.
agents.badge.effectiveTitle: agent.json has no allow-list — worker sees every entry below
agents.configDialog.title: Workboard configuration
agents.configDialog.subtitle: Pool caps apply on the next run acquire. Worker type defaults (echo / llm) re-activate the plugin so changes take effect on the next request.
panel.title: Tasks
panel.admin.title: Workboard
panel.admin.descPart2: on a column to add a task in place. Workers in the pool claim ready tasks and report back with a result summary.
panel.admin.descPart3: v0.2 ships an echo worker (30s sleep + reflect title) so the loop is visible end-to-end; real worker roles land in follow-up PRs.
panel.card.legacyRoleTitle: Legacy: dispatched by kind, not pinned to a slug
panel.execution.rawLogEmpty: No opencode log yet (task hasn't produced one, or the sandbox was torn down).
panel.execution.rawLogUnavailable: Raw log unavailable — this worker doesn't run in a shell sandbox.
panel.modal.sandboxTitle: Microsandbox VM bound to this task. Running while the worker is active, stopped after release (disk preserved), removed when the task is deleted.
panel.modal.title: Title
manifest.configGroups.pool.label: Pool
manifest.config.maxConcurrentRuns.label: Max concurrent worker runs (tenant)
manifest.config.maxConcurrentRuns.descri: Tenant-wide cap on how many worker tasks the pool will run in parallel. 0 (default) means unlimited — each registered worker slot can claim a task. Lower this if you want to throttle total LLM / sandbox load (e.g. set to 2 so only two tasks run at once even though 5 workers are registered).
manifest.config.maxConcurrentRunsPerUser: Max concurrent worker runs per user

Per-user cap on how many of THIS user's tasks can be in flight at once. 0 (default) means unlimited. Useful in shared tenants to keep one user from monopolising the pool — e.g. tenant cap 4, per-user cap 2 means two users can each run two tasks side-by-side. Whichever cap fires first stops further claims.
manifest.config.echo.enabled.label: Enabled
manifest.config.echo.delayMs.label: Simulated execution delay
manifest.config.echo.delayMs.description: How long an echo worker pretends to be busy on each task before marking it done. 0 makes tasks complete instantly.
manifest.config.llm.enabled.label: Enabled
manifest.config.llm.firstResponseSec.des: Hard cap on how long the LLM has to start streaming the very first event of the run. Catches connection-hung scenarios. Set to 0 to disable.
manifest.config.llm.idleSec.label: Idle timeout
manifest.config.llm.idleSec.description: Mid-stream silence cap: if the LLM goes this long without emitting any event, abort the run as stuck. Long enough to allow large `write_file` arguments to stream through.
manifest.config.llm.maxRunSec.label: Max run time
manifest.config.llm.maxRunSec.descriptio: Total wall-clock cap for one task run, across all turns. Protects against runaway loops.
manifest.description: Kanban task board with a worker pool. The orchestrator drops tasks onto the board; workers pick them up, run them, and report back. v0.2 ships an echo worker so the full ready→in-progress→done loop is visible end-to-end; real worker runtimes (qianliyan / luban / xihe / nvwa) land in follow-up PRs as separate plugins.
